_comment: sovereign-os anti-minimization-audit dashboard (R490, R456+). Visualizes the operator-§1g/§1h anti-min instrument that scans for 8 minimization patterns across the codebase. Sourced from sovereign_os_operator_anti_minimization_audit_query_total{verb,pattern,result} (Layer B, SDD-016) emitted by scripts/operator/anti-minimization-audit.py. Operator imports into Grafana via Settings → JSON Model. Closes the future anti-min-dashboard waiver and registers anti-minimization-audit in MODULE_COVERAGE at 3 surfaces (core/cli/dashboard).

panels.title: patterns verb (count)
panels.description: Enumerates the 8 canonical anti-min patterns the operator scans for.

panels.title: scan verb (count)
panels.description: Runs the scan across a pattern (or 'all'). Operator's primary 'where am I shortening the contract?' verb.

panels.title: report verb (count)
panels.description: Operator's clean-sheet pattern×count report — '0 total matches across 8 patterns' is the goal state.

panels.title: waivers verb (count)
panels.description: R474 inline-waiver inventory — every operator-blessed minimization-exception, anchored + justified.

panels.title: anti-min query rate per verb (5m)
panels.description: Per-verb query rate over time. patterns / scan / module / cross-module / report / waivers / selfdef — operator's anti-min-tooling usage pattern.

panels.title: anti-min pattern attention (5m)
panels.description: Per-pattern query rate. todo-no-anchor / empty-stub / skipped-no-followup / surface-gap / doc-gap / mandate-todo / minimize-phrase / partial-status — operator-revealed-preference signal for which pattern is most-watched.

panels.title: anti-min result distribution
panels.description: ok / unknown-pattern / issues — operator-§1g result-class breakdown across all verbs.

panels.title: anti-min current state (verb × pattern × result)
panels.description: Per-(verb, pattern, result) histogram — operator-discoverable: 'which anti-min pattern am I exercising most, and is it clean?'

options.content:
**Operator §1g/§1h verbatim** (R458 — anti-minimization standing rule, sacrosanct):

> "We do not minimize anything."

This instrument IS the source-code enforcement of the standing rule. anti-minimization-audit scans the codebase for the 8 canonical patterns by which a §1g delivery contract can be silently shortened — and refuses to let any of them ship.

**8 canonical patterns (sacrosanct, R456):**

1. `todo-no-anchor` — TODO/FIXME/XXX without an operator-resolvable anchor (issue ID, SDD ref, R-round, waiver marker)
2. `empty-stub` — function/class declared but never implemented, no operator-explicit `# stub-waiver:` marker
3. `skipped-no-followup` — `pytest.skip` / `unittest.skip` without a follow-up reference
4. `surface-gap` — §1g surface listed in MODULE_COVERAGE as FUTURE-class waiver (R478 precision: STRUCTURAL waivers are at ceiling and do NOT fire)
5. `doc-gap` — doc-surface listed in doc-coverage as missing (no readme/sdd/helptext/metric-inventory/mandate-row/man-page for module)
6. `mandate-todo` — mandate row containing TODO/placeholder text
7. `minimize-phrase` — source-code occurrence of 'minimize / minimization / minimized' OUTSIDE a doctrine-echo context (R476 precision: 'anti-minimization' / 'do not minimize' verbatim quotations of the operator rule are doctrine echoes and do NOT fire)
8. `partial-status` — status field set to 'partial' / 'incomplete' / 'in-progress' without an anchor to the round that closes it

**Current state goal (sacrosanct):**

```
── anti-minimization-audit.report (0 total matches across 8 patterns) ──
  ✓ todo-no-anchor         0
  ✓ empty-stub             0
  ✓ skipped-no-followup    0
  ✓ surface-gap            0
  ✓ doc-gap                0
  ✓ mandate-todo           0
  ✓ minimize-phrase        0
  ✓ partial-status         0
```

A SINGLE match across the 8 patterns is the operator-stated emergency: the contract is being silently shortened. The L3 CI gate (R456) FAILS the build if any pattern is non-zero — there is no 'merge with one outstanding'.

**CLI:**

```
sovereign-osctl anti-minimization-audit patterns          # enumerate 8
sovereign-osctl anti-minimization-audit scan [PATTERN]    # cross-codebase
sovereign-osctl anti-minimization-audit module <name>     # per-module
sovereign-osctl anti-minimization-audit cross-module      # heatmap
sovereign-osctl anti-minimization-audit report            # one-screen
sovereign-osctl anti-minimization-audit waivers           # R474 inline
sovereign-osctl anti-minimization-audit selfdef           # cross-repo
```

**Precision filters (R474+R476+R478):**

- `# anti-min-waiver: <anchor> — <rationale>` per-line annotation
- `_MINIMIZE_DOCTRINE_ECHO_RE` — recognizes operator-rule quotations
- `_classify_waiver()` — structural-ceiling vs FUTURE-roadmap distinction

**Metric:** `sovereign_os_operator_anti_minimization_audit_query_total{verb,pattern,result}` (SDD-016 Layer B)

**Source:** R456 shipped the 8-pattern instrument + 7 verbs. R490 (R456+) ships THIS dashboard + registers anti-minimization-audit as a first-class module in MODULE_COVERAGE at 3 surfaces (core/cli/dashboard).

**Standing operator rule (sacrosanct, repeated for emphasis):**

> "We do not minimize anything."

title: sovereign-os anti-minimization-audit
